title: Bird Sound Identification Research
subtitle: Passive acoustic monitoring for avian conservation in Indonesia and Malaysia

highlights: Appointed as Research Team Lead on the avian monitoring project
Sourced and curated recordings from Xeno-canto, focusing on birds endemic and/or endangered in Indonesia, Malaysia, and Borneo island
Defined the target scope: 219 species including 17 Borneo endemics and 18 IUCN-threatened species
Built the full audio preprocessing pipeline: 48 kHz resampling, log-mel spectrogram extraction (96 bins, 96x511 px)
Developed the multi-stage framework: fine-tuning BirdNET v2.4 on the curated dataset of 12,511 recordings + 8,840 negative samples
Achieved +26.7% accuracy and +44.1% macro F1 on the endemic and endangered species subset (McNemar p < 0.05)
Built and deployed the species identification application
Conducted real-world field testing at Bandung Zoo (Indonesia) and Malaysia National Zoo to validate performance under natural acoustic conditions
Presented the paper at ICITACEE 2025 (Semarang, Indonesia)

publications.title: Deep Learning for Bird Identification Using Sound: Enhancing Avian Monitoring and Conservation in Indonesia and Malaysia

highlights: Assisting lecturers in preparing a journal submission to PeerJ for the extended research findings
Improving sound source separation using MixIT (Mixture Invariant Training) combined with unsupervised learning models
Targeting more robust performance on overlapping bird calls and high-noise passive monitoring recordings
